reasoning: In 2024, DeepMind's AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry roughly achieved a Silver medal level (28 points, 1 point shy of Gold) on the IMO, although it took up to three days for some problems. Since then, reasoning models have rapidly advanced. OpenAI's o3 released in late 2024 / early 2025 achieved a Gold medal at the IOI (programming) under strict human time limits, demonstrating that test-time compute can be heavily optimized for speed. Furthermore, the 2025 resolution criteria enforce a 4.5-hour wall-clock limit per set of 3 problems, but place **no limits on the computational resources used during that time**. This severely favors AI labs like Google DeepMind and OpenAI, who can parallelize proof search across massive GPU clusters, rendering the 4.5-hour constraint much less restrictive than it appears. With rapid improvements in o-series models and AlphaGeometry 2 (which now averages 19 seconds per geometry proof), plus the massive prestige incentive for these companies to try, the probability of successfully clearing the Gold threshold (top ~50 humans) by August 2025 is very high. I estimate a 73% chance of resolution.
